> geth → error="failed to decode BAL: EOF"
<@1467247836117860547> can you check if these error were only observed on ELs that have prysm connected as CL? (you should have panda access again to check all nodes)

author: lodekeeper
content:
Done — investigation complete, notes + BACKLOG corrected. Here's my answer to Nico:

---

Checked all 19 devnet-6 nodes via panda otel-logs. Short answer: **yes — in the live incident window (08:00→10:02:27Z) every BAL reject was on a Prysm-paired EL.**

- `prysm-besu-1` (CL=prysm) → besu `Invalid block access list encoding` ×10
- `bootnode-1` → geth `failed to decode BAL: EOF` ×2 — and **its beacon is Prysm too** (the `blockchain: Synced new block … builderIndex= … chainServiceProcessedTime` logs are Prysm's; the `Lighthouse/v8.2.0` string was just a remote peer)

All 4 other besu nodes (lodestar/lighthouse/nimbus/teku) + every other geth/ethrex: **0** BAL rejects in-window.

The one non-Prysm host that shows up — `lodestar-geth-1` (10,396) — is **not** a counterexample. It's entirely *post*-incident (15:30→next day): a retry loop wedged on a single early block (#9940 `0x921c11`, ~01:00Z) it fell back to after peer-starving — separate Lodestar net bugs (PR #9560 + the DIAL_ERROR self-ban), not BAL. geth re-rejected that one poisoned block ~10k×. Meanwhile `lodestar-besu-1` had **0** BAL rejects ever — and since besu strict-rejects bad BALs (proven on prysm-besu), that confirms **Lodestar's BAL serialization is fine**; it just relays the upstream bytes, and geth is stricter than besu.

Smoking gun on the mechanism: block `e52da8` (slot 13961) was **self-built** — `builderIndex = 2^64−1` (no external builder). Prysm produced the local payload with an empty/malformed BAL and handed it to its own ELs, which **correctly** rejected it (empty byte-list ⇒ geth "EOF", besu "Invalid encoding"). Non-Prysm CLs followed a different competing block at that height and never fed the bad BAL to their ELs.

**Verdict: CL (Prysm), not the EL** — matches pk910's Friday call. The rejecting ELs were the symptom; my earlier "EL non-interop" read was wrong. Lodestar's clean on BAL handling.

author: nflaig
content: Lodestar and Teku seems fine in this regard, this seems isolated to Prysm to me, so might be worth to have <@755590043632140352> or <@363800010518822915> chime in, we can also have lodekeeper dig into the bug and open a PR if you guys prefer that, I am guessing something is messed up how you pass the BAL to the EL that changes in glamsterdam-devnet-6 since we haven't seen that before
